title: agropyron repens subsp. dumetorum
intro: Agropyron repens subsp. dumetorum, commonly known as couch grass or quack grass, is a subspecies of perennial grass belonging to the Poaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Agropyron repens subsp. dumetorum include relieving digestive issues, reducing fever, and soothing respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat various health conditions, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy.
